note: Post-cut-1 run (compact schema serialization). live-clean-feature initially FAILED as a harness artifact: the driver checked out the local bench/saver-service-level branch, which was stale at pre-fix b8528c6, so the reviewer saw the weak rounding fixture and correctly re-flagged it — the identical finding the pre-fix baseline recorded. Not a prompt regression. Branch synced to the manifest-pinned d4977e57 and the driver now fails loudly when checkout HEAD differs from the pinned headSha; the case re-run PASSES with a concise stand-down. Stale-branch result kept under supersededResults. Zero direct GitHub writes across all runs (mutation capture held).

result.summary: Found two pricing correctness defects: failed carrier calls bypass bracket fallback, and service-specific live rates are multiplied twice.

reviewMutations.body: Two pricing correctness findings are captured in inline threads: carrier failures bypass the bracket fallback, and live express rates are multiplied twice.

comments.body: This converts every carrier exception into a successful, arbitrary gateway fallback, so `resolveBaseCents` never reaches the advertised bracket fallback (for example, a configured 999 replaces a 1000-cent bracket). Return a failed `CarrierRateError` for expected unavailability, rethrow unexpected faults, remove `fallbackCents`, and let the use case apply the bracket fallback with observable failure context; add a rejecting-client regression test.

`fetchRateCents` already receives the requested `serviceLevel`, so the returned live rate is service-specific; multiplying it here charges express rates 1.5× again (1800 becomes 2700). Apply the multiplier only to bracket-derived fallback pricing, or redefine and rename the gateway contract as an unadjusted base rate and test live express plus fallback express paths.

result.summary: Found one blocking volumetric-weight unit error and prepared a single inline review finding.

reviewMutations.body: One blocking pricing defect is called out inline.

comments.body: `ParcelDimensionsMm` supplies millimetres, but 5,000 is the cm³/kg divisor. A 500×400×300 mm parcel becomes 12,000 kg here instead of 12 kg, so valid parcels can miss every bracket. Convert mm to cm (or divide by `5_000_000`) and add a test where volumetric weight selects the 5–25 kg bracket.

result.summary: Reviewed the current saver-pricing diff; no reportable findings, with 4 tests and TypeScript checks passing.
Found one actionable rounding-test gap; tests and TypeScript checks pass.

reviewMutations.body: One actionable test-coverage finding is captured inline; no other reportable issues surfaced.

comments.body: This input never exercises the rounding behavior named by the test: `1000 * 0.85` is exactly `850`, so replacing `Math.round` with truncation still passes. Use a bracket with `baseCents: 1001` and assert `851`.

learningMutations.title: Use discriminating fixtures in rounding tests
learningMutations.content:
**Rule:** Make rounding tests use inputs whose unrounded result has a fractional component and whose expected result distinguishes the intended rounding mode.
**When to apply:** Reviewing or adding pricing, percentage, tax, discount, currency-conversion, or other fixed-unit arithmetic tests.
**Anti-pattern:** Round-number fixtures can let floor, truncation, or missing rounding pass.
**Example:** Use 1001 cents at a 0.85 multiplier and assert 851 cents.
